desc_short: Stabilize the park. Enforce the border. Make the canyon compliant.
desc_long: From the maps of Liberty City, Coffin Canyon is not chaos. It is a Forbidden National Park: a vast, federally claimed preserve set aside for study, stabilization, and eventual development under the authority of the United States of Liberty. Entry without permit is illegal. Resource extraction without oversight is theft. Armed presence without sanction is sedition. That anyone disputes this is unfortunate. The Liberty Corps exists to resolve that misunderstanding.
history: For decades, Coffin Canyon was quarantined, studied from afar, and written off as too costly to tame. That has changed. Silver veins demand oversight. Thyr demands control. Monsters demand management. The Liberty Corps has been dispatched to secure the park, enforce federal exclusion, and prepare Coffin Canyon for responsible use — checkpoints, forts, railheads, controlled extraction, and where necessary, lethal intervention.

core_values: Containment, arrest, or removal of unlawful presences
what_they_fight_for: A stabilized, productive Coffin Canyon under federal control
An orderly battlefield — quiet, compliant, logged
what_they_fight_against: Uncontrolled monsters as hazards and occult contamination

reputation.allies_see_them_as: Immaculate professionals bringing stability, logistics, and security to a hostile preserve
reputation.enemies_see_them_as: Occupation forces — paperwork-backed violence with steam and steel
reputation.monsters_see_them_as: Cages, chains, and gunfire — jurisdiction made physical
reputation.the_canyon_sees_them_as: Flags in the dust. Checkpoints at the throat. A claim made real.

how_they_see_others.monster_rangers: Well-meaning but misguided. Their sentimentality undermines containment and emboldens threats. Tolerated — for now.
how_they_see_others.shine_riders: Criminal trespassers. Poachers. Vandals. Arrest on sight.
how_they_see_others.monsterologists: Once sanctioned partners under classified agreements — now untrustworthy and unwelcome trespassers.

faction_identity.on_monsters: Monsters are illegal occult foreigners — unregistered entities operating inside a federally restricted National Park without permit, citizenship, or recognized legal standing. Some may be temporarily tolerated for study or sanctioned use. Most are subject to removal, neutralization, or classified disposition. Liberty does not negotiate with unlawful presences. Liberty cleans its borders.
faction_identity.uncle_liberty: There are contingencies the Corps does not discuss publicly. One of them is Uncle Liberty: the first President of the United States of Liberty, sustained beyond mortality by steam, steel, and duty. Uncle Liberty is not deployed lightly. He is a national response asset.

faction_mechanics.signature_ability_description: Liberty Corps wins by turning resistance into compliance: restrain, arrest, transport, and extract. They control space with pins, lockdowns, and disciplined advance — then score through removal and zone stability.
faction_mechanics.playstyle: Zone-control enforcers who secure areas, expand outward, and escalate deliberately. They prefer tidy battlefields: pinned lanes, denied reactions, constrained movement, and arrests backed by steam hardware and command authority.
strengths: Excellent movement denial (pin, lockdown, grapple, suppress)
Durable organized front line supported by technicians
Strong command aura and morale reliability
Scoring through removal and extraction rather than pure attrition
weaknesses: Can be outmaneuvered by extreme mobility and deep infiltration
Arrest plans collapse if key control pieces fall
Less flexible in highly mystical play without dedicated counters
High-value assets become liabilities if lost early

preferred_states.reason: The Corps' ideal end-state: cleared lanes, enforced exclusion, and the battlefield made compliant.
Stable controlled zones reward disciplined expansion and checkpoint play.

neutral_states.reason: The Corps treats it as under-policed territory — more paperwork, more arrests.
Operationally unpleasant but the Corps fields respirators and procedures for toxic environments.

opposed_states.reason: Uncontrolled ecology and monster proliferation undermines stabilization.
Haunts and occult contamination disrupt procedure and complicate containment.
Competing sovereignty and mystic law interferes with federal authority.
Monsterology's exploitation turns the park into a scarred theft-zone hostile to lawful oversight.
Automation-heavy dead zones reduce the Corps' leverage against living hazards.

ideal_scenarios: Escort of survey teams and rail crews
challenging_scenarios: Deeply mystical battlefields without clear lines
Monster-swarm scenarios that overwhelm arrest capacity
Fog and Haunt-heavy boards that disrupt command

faction_features.name: Federal Exclusion Jurisdiction
faction_features.effect: Liberty Corps treats Coffin Canyon as a Forbidden National Park under federal control. Operations prioritize stabilization, eviction, and removal of unlawful presences.

faction_features.name: Arrest & Extraction Doctrine
faction_features.effect: Liberty Corps specializes in restraining and removing targets rather than simply destroying them. Units with grapple, binding_rig, and lockdown excel at creating arrests; transport units convert arrests into secure removals and scoring.

faction_features.name: Procedure Is Patriotism
faction_features.effect: Command presence (authority, inspire, signal_orders) keeps units coordinated and resistant to morale disruption — so long as the chain of command remains intact.

weapons_note: Unit costs include standard-issue weapons. Weapon effects are listed under weapon_properties for each unit.

units.name: Patriot Asset
units.lore: Cheap bodies in cheap uniforms — pressed into service, promised glory, issued a whistle and a pair of cuffs.
units.tactics: Flood lanes, contest objectives, and enable arrests. They die easily but cost little — treat them as mobile obstacles that slow the enemy and enable your real pieces. Cold-Iron Handcuffs makes their assist on arrests meaningfully harder to break. Citation Ledger improves Interact actions for scenarios with objective handling.

optional_upgrades.name: Federal Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Cold-Iron Handcuffs
optional_upgrades.desc_short: When this unit helps apply a Restrained or Captured state, the target must score 2+ successes to Break Free instead of 1. Titans ignore this upgrade.

optional_upgrades.name: Citation Ledger
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per activation, when this model makes an Interact action tied to Seize, Arrest, Evidence, or Objective handling, gain +1 die for that Interact roll.

units.name: Steam Constable
units.lore: Uniformed enforcers in wool coats and iron helmets. Steam packs drive piston-fists built for arrests, not duels.
units.tactics: Frontline control. Grapple locks targets for arrests and prevents breakthroughs. Shield Wall rewards clustering — run two or three Constables together for a wall that is surprisingly hard to push through. Cold-Iron Restraint Kit upgrades a Grapple into a full Lockdown, preventing Disengage entirely. Iron Slab Plating is worth considering if you expect sustained AP fire.

optional_upgrades.name: Federal Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Iron Slab Plating
optional_upgrades.desc_short: +1 Defense, -1" Move. Gain armored (reduce damage taken by 1, minimum 0).

optional_upgrades.name: Cold-Iron Restraint Kit
optional_upgrades.desc_short: While engaged with a Grappled or Restrained enemy, that enemy cannot Disengage or use free moves (lockdown). Ends if this model becomes Shaken or is forcibly moved.

units.name: Field Technician
units.lore: Cable-haulers and wrench-hands who keep Liberty Corps machinery alive in places it was never meant to operate.
units.tactics: Support backbone. Keep the Technician adjacent to your Black Mariah or Troll-Howitzer — Repair keeps your most expensive pieces functional under fire. Kick the Machine removes Hex or Suppression from those same units, which is critical against Monsterology or Shine Rider debuff play. Portable Jamming Coil shuts down a 6" bubble of Mystic abilities — extremely powerful against Conjure Guards and Crystal Whisperers.

optional_upgrades.name: Federal Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Spare Parts Satchel
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Start with 2 Spare Parts tokens. Each Repair action may spend a token instead of consuming an external Spare Parts source.

optional_upgrades.name: Portable Jamming Coil
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Enemy units within 6" cannot use Hex, Charm, or other Mystic special abilities.

units.name: Liberty Corps Detective
units.lore: Plain clothes, keen eyes, documented everything. The warrant is already drafted — signed, stamped, inevitable.
units.tactics: Priority marking and lane control. Third Eye Vision reveals Stealth units, which is crucial against Crow Rangers, Canyon Jackals, and Doom Mimes. Mark Target then concentrates all fire on the most dangerous piece. Pinning Shot on the pistol locks down targets for arrests. Case File stacks Suppressed on top of Mark Target in one action — a devastating combination against Leaders.

optional_upgrades.name: Federal Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Cold-Iron Handcuffs
optional_upgrades.desc_short: When this unit helps apply a Restrained or Captured state, the target must score 2+ successes to Break Free instead of 1. Titans ignore this upgrade.

optional_upgrades.name: Case File: Pattern Analysis
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game, when you use mark_target, also apply Suppressed to that target.

units.name: Warrant Officer
units.lore: The best at what they do: steam-assisted reflexes, reinforced armor, and a perfect arrest record.
units.tactics: Mobile arrest specialist. Fast plus Grapple plus Binding Rig is the cleanest capture package in the faction. Move 7" plus Fast means the Officer covers ground quickly — use them to run down fleeing high-value targets or cut off escape routes. Cold-Iron Handcuffs makes every arrest they set up extremely hard to escape from. Heroic Grit is worth the points on a unit that regularly puts itself in danger.

optional_upgrades.name: Federal Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Cold-Iron Handcuffs
optional_upgrades.desc_short: When this unit helps apply a Restrained or Captured state, the target must score 2+ successes to Break Free instead of 1. Titans ignore this upgrade.

optional_upgrades.name: Heroic Grit
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game: ignore the first wound suffered.

units.name: Marksman Contractor
units.lore: Contract shooters attached to enforcement columns. Their job isn't justice — it's results, recorded and delivered.
units.tactics: Delete or cripple key pieces at 20" range. Brutal Pierce ignores 2 Defense — Brine Divers, Pluguglies, and armored units are no longer safe. Deadeye rerolls all failed dice once per game; save it for the shot that needs to land. Camouflage Mantle in Cover makes the Contractor very difficult to target back. Shredder Rounds is a once-per-game Savage Pierce attack that ignores 3 Defense — use it on the most armored thing on the board.

optional_upgrades.name: Federal Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Camouflage Mantle
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain stealth while in Cover. If you leave Cover, stealth ends until you re-enter.

optional_upgrades.name: Shredder Rounds
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game: declare before a ranged attack. That attack upgrades pierce to savage_pierce (ignore 3 Defense) for this attack only.

units.name: Riot Harmonist
units.lore: A brass-backed crowd-control officer. Used for signaling, dispersal, and breaking resolve with weaponized sound.
units.tactics: Area denial plus morale pressure. The Bombardion's Sonic Blast ignores Cover and hits everything in a 3" radius — ideal against clustered units. Audio Suppression neutralizes sound-based abilities nearby (Inspire, Calamity Parade, Radiomancer Jam). Signal Orders provides a free 3" move to any friendly unit per activation. Position behind the front line and use Signal Orders to give late-deploying units a head start.

optional_upgrades.name: Federal Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Smoke Canisters
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain smoke_screen as a Main Action, once per game.

optional_upgrades.name: Discipline Cadence
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain authority aura: friendly units within 6" ignore Suppression.

units.name: Liberty Corps Judge
units.lore: In Liberty Corps jurisdiction, Judges hold executive power in the field. They prosecute, sentence, and enforce — cleanly, publicly, and without appeal.
units.tactics: Command anchor. Authority keeps the line immune to Suppression. Inspire adds dice to critical rolls across the force. Pass Judgment permanently debuffs one enemy — pick the unit that will cause the most damage across the whole game, ideally a Hero or Leader. Seal of Office adds Intimidate for morale plays. Writ of Eviction denies reactions after Interact actions, which prevents enemies from breaking arrests or contesting objective grabs.

optional_upgrades.name: Federal Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Seal of Office
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain intimidate as a Main Action: force Morale test on enemy within 6" and LoS. No effect on Titans.

optional_upgrades.name: Writ of Eviction
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per round, after this model resolves an Interact action, one enemy within 6" cannot take Reaction actions until end of round.

units.name: Black Mariah
units.lore: A steam-powered armored prison wagon. It doesn't bring help. It brings jurisdiction.
units.tactics: Your scoring engine and denial piece. The whole arrest plan is worthless without the Mariah — it converts Restrained enemies into secure removals and score. Protect it at all costs. Reinforced Cell Bars ensures that even if it is destroyed, the cargo at least enters play Shaken and Pinned rather than fully functional. Steam Booster is a once-per-game burst of 4" that lets the Mariah reach a fleeing target or cut off an escape route.

optional_upgrades.name: Federal Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Reinforced Cell Bars
optional_upgrades.desc_short: If this unit is destroyed, captured models are still released but enter play Shaken and Pinned.

optional_upgrades.name: Steam Booster
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game: after this model moves, it may immediately move an additional 4".

units.name: Classified Asset: Troll-Howitzer
units.lore: A restrained monster fused to artillery hardware. It fires because it hurts. It exists because paperwork said it could.
units.tactics: Deterrence through escalation. Every shot forces Morale tests on everyone within 12" — including your own units — so plan positioning carefully. Stabilizer Spikes prevent enemy abilities from displacing it, which matters against Grapple-heavy forces. Reinforced Restraint Harness gives one free knockback ignore per round. Do not lose it before Round 4 — Mismanaged Asset is a significant VP penalty that the enemy will absolutely try to exploit.

optional_upgrades.name: Liberty Federal Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Stabilizer Spikes
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain anchor: cannot be moved by enemy abilities or effects. Ignores knockback and displacement.

optional_upgrades.name: Reinforced Restraint Harness
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per round: ignore the first forced movement or repositioning from an enemy ability.

units.name: Uncle Liberty
units.lore: The first President of the United States of Liberty. A living symbol of national resolve, sustained beyond mortality by steam, steel, and duty. He is not deployed lightly. He is a national response asset.
units.tactics: Catastrophic closer. When he arrives, it stops being a negotiation. Savage Impact deals 3 automatic hits on a charge — lead with a 3"+ move into the most dangerous thing on the board. Regeneration keeps him on the table turn after turn. Unyielding prevents any displacement effects. Overdrive Directive gives a bonus action at cost of 1 Quality — use it at full health for maximum efficiency.

special_rules.national_response_asset: Scenario or roster rules may restrict Uncle Liberty's inclusion (campaign escalation, high point thresholds, or special missions).
special_rules.insane_contingency: If core rules include Uncle Liberty becoming uncontrollable, apply those rules consistently from this file.

optional_upgrades.name: Antique Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: An old and ratty gas mask, in Red, White, and Brass. Ignores Coffin Cough and other airborne dangers.

optional_upgrades.name: Steam Governor
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game: reduce incoming damage by 2 before applying reductions (minimum 0).

optional_upgrades.name: Overdrive Directive
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game: after Uncle Liberty resolves an Attack or Interact action, he may immediately take one additional action. After the second action resolves, he loses 1 Quality.
